item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with an image that's cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and is sourced as follows:

Title: 1865-new.png
Author(s): Jože Gal, Elogee FishTruck
Release year: 2020
Note: Created by Elogee FishTruck as an edited version of the image below

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: Public Domain
Title: Gradbišče hidroelektrarne Ožbalt 1958, potapljač.jpg
Author: Jože Gal

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's nothing here to really get my interest or attention. -1

item.title: STAFF POST OPEN- Crosslink Proposal
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Show Reason
Hide Reason

A crosslink is being proposed to SCP-1608 due to the relative relation of the articles, and the Authors headcanon with respect to 1865-2 origin. For the crosslink, we would add in a footnote with the proposed link.

Editing this:

this surface is dotted with nodules containing multiple aquatic biological entities referred to as SCP-1865-2.

To this:

this surface is dotted with nodules containing multiple aquatic biological entities1 referred to as SCP-1865-2.

Footnotes
1. Consist with those found within the interior of SCP-1608

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Remind me of a diving suited blob from X-Com 2.

I'm not sure what's going on (other than it's diving suit overtaken by colony of aquatic fauna) or why it's called TaoTao, but the thing is creepy (then again, deep diving suit always give that vibe) and amusing enough to earn my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't really understand what makes this one anomalous. Is it that all of the beings inside are working together, or just that they're undiscovered beings?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought the image of the botched handshake was humorous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the line [These dividers in these section act as main points for valves] should be changed to [The dividers in these sections act as main points for valves] or simply [These sections act as main points for valves]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea of a bunch of fish and sea creatures getting together in a diving suit to basically do the opposite of what we do with them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just don't get it… it's a squid and a bunch of other poorly-defined aquatic creatures that for some bizarre reason occupy a diving suit. It gave me nothing to get excited about in the least.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not extremely interesting and I've seen this concept before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah I just think it's silly that they have a division there. Like SCP Foundation EASTER ISLAND labs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How does it fit inside the container they use for transport? Do they have to take the organisms out of the suit? I don't think they'd appreciate that, and the suit's too big to fit inside the container.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pressure in pascals, please, not psi.

Otherwise, I'm not sure I get it, so no vote for the time being.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could use a little polish, but I love the premise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This seems to be lifted directly from UnLunDun by China Mieville. The whole bit about a diving suit puppeteered from the inside by a group of aquatic creatures, and the "ambassador to the surface" bit, are the same; as far as I can see the only addition is a more detailed description of the inner mechanics of the suit than Mieville gave.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How I felt about this. Kind of reminds me of Octodad, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1865 is to be contained inside of a humanoid containment unit that has been immersed in seawater.

I think you mean that the containment unit is filled with seawater, not immersed in seawater.

Instances of SCP-1865-2 are lodged within breached suit sections. All instances of SCP-1865 are deep ocean organisms.

The article doesn't mention the (apparent) species of the -2 found in the chest, arms/legs, or hands/feet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very neat idea, although it's Octopunch as an SCP. (I cannot get a link at this moment because TFwiki.)

Anyway, I think there are a few things in here that could use smoothing out (the cephalopod, for instance, should probably be -1, since that designation is never used but -2 is), but this mostly just needs a spit-polish to be really good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice job - the idea of the 'ambassador to the surface' comes out much more strongly than in the draft. I do agree with MyFatedMethod that the contents of the various body parts could be presented more cleanly - perhaps with a table?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm actually not sure what to think of that. Like, there's literally Foundation personnel stationed in Saipan on the off-chance that anomalous activity happens to a remote island of 40,000 people? I would think it more likely that there's a Foundation operative in the United States federal government whose responsibility is American holdings in the Oceania/Pacific Islands region that's the first responder to stuff like this.

Is what you're implying that there's something inherently anomalous about Saipan and/or its governance?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1. I like how it's not even necessarily hostile, in my view it was just trying to introduce itself and the network of sea creatures in the hand of the suit is just clumsy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Foundation personnel embedded in the Saipan government

No one finds this interesting?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it and planned an upvote until WHOA awkward body parts description, and it became tiresome. No vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … Ok. You can have an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't even

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The biggest question that haunted my reading of it was "how did the thing get the diving suit?"

That question is compelling enough for me to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The suit is divided into 6 sections
Chest:
Arms/Legs:
Abdomen:
Hands/Feet:
Helmet:

I only see five parts here. Unless you mean the 'divided into six sections, including the head, arms, chest, and legs', which would make for only 4 sections, unless each arm and each leg is a different section. Could you clarify whatyou mean?

Otherwise, don't really get it, don't know how to feel about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A collaboration between me and faminepulse. Famine also shooped the image for this article.
